creator: axel.burwitz@arcor.de
text:
Hi,

meanwhile.....

for various reasons I had to make a fresh complete install, I am now on RC2...

I updated ports tree with cvsup and retried.

A "pkg_add -r linux-realplayer" didn't work:
"freebsd# pkg_add -r linux-realplayer
Error: FTP Unable to get 
ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/ports/i386/packages-6.2-release/Latest/linux-realplayer.tbz: 
File unavailable (e.g., file not found, no access)
pkg_add: unable to 
fetch 'ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/ports/i386/packages-6.2-release/Latest/linux-realplayer.tbz' 
by URL"

but  a make install clean from ports directory ran as smooth as silk!    
(RealPlayer-10.0.8.805-20060718.i586.rpm)



Thanks for help! Next time I will think of port updating.....


BTW:
>Also, what's up with the freebsd.debian hostname? :)

that "debian" is only historically the domain name for my various PC's; comes 
from the time when I used to work with Debian. Later I had Gentoo Linux on 
three PCs, but now first choice is FreeBSD!

:-)

regards
Axel
